creator: koobs@FreeBSD.org
text:
As FreeBSD continues to evolve its coordination, management and ongoing presence on IRC, and having now formalised its group contact management with Freenode staff, it is now necessary to begin to consolidate and establish a consistent baseline experience across our our channels.

The FreeBSD IRC channel namespace is #freebsd-* (but may also include #freebsd*, pending a response from freenode) and we have obtained a list (from freenode staff) of existing channels currently registered in that namespace.

At a high-level (see sub-tasks for details), this issue aims for the following short-term outcomes, for any and all FreeBSD existing and future channels that fall under the group registration:

1)  Owned by and manageable by the IRC Admin team and its registered IRC account(s).

2) For existing channels, owners/operators/moderators are aware of and agree to (1), or those channels are de-registered or moved to an alternative non-registered namespace.

3) That the process and all requirements for registering channels are documented in a publicly accessible form (Wiki)

4) That the utility and value of existing channels is maximised. (read: duplicate or similar channels merged, consolidated, etc)

5) That there is a consistent baseline configuration applies. Eg: channel modes, topics, notices, settings, contact information for user support/abuse
